pi_items.source: Task setup script confirms directory structure
Task setup script and export_result.sh confirm path
Task description and physics context - 2.5 m/s is realistic for tidal stream turbines
https://www.ittc.info/media/4048/75-02-01-03.pdf - seawater kinematic viscosity ranges 1.0-1.5×10⁻⁶ m²/s at typical temperatures

Physics calculation: P = 0.5 × ρ × A × v³ × Cp with ρ=1025 kg/m³, A=78.54 m² (5m radius), v=2.5 m/s. For Cp=0.238 (minimum needed for 150 kW), this is below typical tidal turbine efficiency (0.37-0.48)
Physics calculation: 400 kW would require Cp=0.636, exceeding the theoretical Betz limit of 0.593. Realistic maximum with Cp=0.48 is 301.9 kW
Physics calculation: Air simulation with ρ=1.225 kg/m³ yields ~0.3 kW at Cp=0.4. 5 kW is a reasonable threshold to distinguish air from water simulation

pi_items.verified_value: Re=1,000,000 is consistent with V=2.5 m/s, ν=1.0e-6 m²/s, L=0.4m (tip chord)
pi_items.source: Reynolds number formula Re = V×L/ν: 1,000,000 = 2.5 × 0.4 / 1.0e-6. XFoil analysis at Re=1,000,000 with Ncrit=9 is standard practice (https://airfoiltools.com/polar/details?polar=xf-n0012-il-1000000)

pi_items.verified_value: NACA 63-818 is a real airfoil from the NACA 6-series family
pi_items.source: https://www.researchgate.net/figure/NACA-4418-NACA-63-818-and-NACA-63-218-hydrofoils_fig5_289531220 - confirmed as real airfoil used in turbine research

pi_items.verified_value: Geometry is consistent with typical tidal turbine designs
pi_items.source: Task description - consistent with research on blade design (https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0029801819307723)

pi_items.metadata_value: QBlade can simulate tidal turbines with custom fluid properties
pi_items.verified_value: QBlade is confirmed to support tidal turbine simulation with custom density/viscosity
pi_items.source: https://docs.qblade.org/index.html and https://qblade.org/ - QBlade supports blade design, airfoil analysis via XFoil, and turbine simulation with custom fluid properties

privileged_info_summary: QBlade is legitimate turbine simulation software capable of analyzing custom airfoils (NACA 63-818) via integrated XFoil at Re=1,000,000 (Ncrit=9) and simulating blade performance in seawater (density 1025 kg/m³, kinematic viscosity 1.0e-6 m²/s). For a 5-meter radius tidal blade at 2.5 m/s flow velocity, realistic power output ranges 190-302 kW (Cp=0.30-0.48). The air simulation baseline (~0.3 kW) allows verification that users simulate seawater, not air. The task uses standard engineering parameters and realistic blade geometry (root chord ~0.8m, tip chord ~0.4m).
